question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here is a complete breakdown of the `charmcraft.yaml` structure for a Kubernetes charm, based on the [official Charmcraft documentation](https://documentation.ubuntu.com/charmcraft/stable/reference/files/charmcraft-yaml-file/).

---

## Complete `charmcraft.yaml` Example — Kubernetes Charm

```yaml
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# REQUIRED FIELDS
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
name: my-k8s-app           # Sets the .charm filename and `juju deploy <name>`
type: charm                # Must be "charm"
base: ubuntu@24.04         # OS for building AND running the charm

summary: A one-line summary of my Kubernetes application.  # Max 78 chars

description: |
  A longer, multi-line description of the charm that will
  be shown on Charmhub. Explain what the application does
  and why someone would want to deploy it.

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# PLATFORM / ARCHITECTURE
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
platforms:
  amd64:          # Build/run on amd64
  # arm64:        # Uncomment to also support ARM64

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# KUBERNETES-SPECIFIC: declare cloud requirements
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
assumes:
  - k8s-api          # Only deploy to a Kubernetes cloud
  - juju >= 3.3      # Minimum Juju version

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# CONTAINERS (required for sidecar K8s charms)
# One entry per container in the pod.
# Must match a resource of type oci-image below.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
containers:
  app:
    resource: app-image          # References the oci-image resource below
    mounts:
      - storage: app-data        # References the storage entry below
        location: /var/lib/app   # Mount path inside the container
    # uid: 1000   # Requires Juju 3.5+ (0-999 or >10000 only)
    # gid: 1000

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# RESOURCES: OCI image for each container above
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
resources:
  app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the main application container.
    upstream-source: ubuntu/my-app:latest   # Optional default registry source

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# STORAGE: persistent volumes
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
storage:
  app-data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent storage for application data.
    location: /var/lib/app
    minimum-size: 1G

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# CONFIG: user-facing configuration options
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: "INFO"
      description: "Log verbosity level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR)."
    replicas:
      type: int
      default: 1
      description: "Number of application replicas."
    enable-tls:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: "Enable TLS termination."
    db-secret:
      type: secret                 # Value must be a Juju secret URI
      description: "Juju secret URI containing DB credentials."

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# RELATIONS
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
provides:
  http:
    interface: http               # Other charms can relate to us over HTTP

requires:
  postgresql:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: false               # App will NOT start until this is satisfied
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1                      # Only one ingress relation allowed

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-k8s-app-peers   # Used for leader-election / peer coordination

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# ACTIONS: operator-invoked commands
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  rotate-password:
    description: Rotate the application's admin password.
    params:
      username:
        type: string
        description: "The user whose password to rotate."
    required:
      - username
    additionalProperties: false

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEVICES: GPU / hardware (optional)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
devices:
  gpu:
    type: nvidia.com/gpu
    description: NVIDIA GPU for inference workloads.
    countmin: 1
    countmax: 4

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# CHARM LIBRARIES
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0.39"

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# PRIVILEGE LEVEL (Juju 3.6+)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
charm-user: non-root    # root | sudoer | non-root

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# BUILD CONFIGURATION
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
parts:
  my-k8s-app:
    plugin: charm
    charm-python-packages:
      - psycopg2-binary
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# METADATA / LINKS
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────
title: My Kubernetes Application    # Human-readable name (distinct from `name`)

links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-k8s-app/12345
  source: https://github.com/example/my-k8s-app-operator
  issues: https://github.com/example/my-k8s-app-operator/issues
  contact: maintainer@example.com
```

---

## Key Rules for Kubernetes Charms

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`assumes: k8s-api`** | Prevents accidental deployment to machine clouds |
| **`containers` ↔ `resources`** | Every container needs a matching `oci-image` resource |
| **`resource` XOR `bases`** | Each container uses *either* `resource:` (OCI) *or* `bases:` (ROCK), never both |
| **`mounts` → `storage`** | Every mount must reference a named entry under `storage:` |
| **`uid`/`gid` values** | Only `0–999` or `>10000` are valid (1000–9999 are reserved) |
| **`optional: false`** on `requires` | Blocks workload startup until the relation is satisfied |
| **`platforms:`** replaces `bases:`| `bases:` is deprecated; use `platforms:` in new charms |
| **`charmcraft.yaml` is the only file to edit** | Charmcraft auto-generates `metadata.yaml`, `config.yaml`, and `actions.yaml` from it |
